name: gcloud
context: Install, configure, and use the gcloud CLI to manage all Google Cloud Platform services from the terminal
info: This skill covers the gcloud CLI, the primary command-line tool for Google Cloud Platform: installation, authentication, project and configuration management, and common command patterns across all GCP services. gcloud is organized into command groups (compute, storage, functions, iam, projects, etc.) that mirror GCP service boundaries. Agents use this skill to set up credentials, configure defaults, and execute infrastructure operations. The gcloud CLI also includes gsutil (Cloud Storage) and bq (BigQuery) as companion tools. See [[gcp/iam]] for IAM-specific gcloud commands, [[gcp/compute]] for compute commands, [[gcp/storage]] for storage commands, and [[gcp/functions]] for function deployment commands.
body:
# gcloud CLI

The gcloud CLI is the primary command-line tool for interacting with Google Cloud Platform. It manages authentication, project configuration, and resources across all GCP services.

## Installation

```bash
# macOS (via Homebrew)
brew install --cask google-cloud-sdk

# macOS / Linux (official installer)
curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash
exec -l $SHELL  # restart shell to pick up PATH

# Debian/Ubuntu
apt-get install apt-transport-https ca-certificates gnupg curl
curl https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | apt-key add -
echo "deb https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt cloud-sdk main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/google-cloud-sdk.list
apt-get update && apt-get install google-cloud-cli

# Docker
docker run -it google/cloud-sdk:latest gcloud version

# Verify installation
gcloud version
```

## Authentication

```bash
# Interactive login (opens browser)
gcloud auth login

# Set Application Default Credentials (for client libraries)
gcloud auth application-default login

# Activate a service account (non-interactive, CI/CD)
gcloud auth activate-service-account \
  --key-file=service-account-key.json

# List authenticated accounts
gcloud auth list

# Print access token (for use with curl/REST)
gcloud auth print-access-token

# Revoke credentials
gcloud auth revoke
```

## Configuration

gcloud uses named configurations to switch between projects, accounts, and regions.

```bash
# Set default project
gcloud config set project my-project-id

# Set default compute region and zone
gcloud config set compute/region us-central1
gcloud config set compute/zone us-central1-a

# View current configuration
gcloud config list

# Create a named configuration (for multi-project work)
gcloud config configurations create work-prod
gcloud config set project prod-project-id
gcloud config set account admin@company.com
gcloud config set compute/region us-east1

# Switch between configurations
gcloud config configurations activate work-dev

# List all configurations
gcloud config configurations list

# Use a different project for one command
gcloud compute instances list --project=other-project
```

## Command Structure

```
gcloud <group> <subgroup> <command> [--flags]

Examples:
  gcloud compute instances list
  gcloud storage buckets create gs://my-bucket
  gcloud functions deploy my-func --gen2
  gcloud iam service-accounts create my-sa
  gcloud projects describe my-project
```

### Command Groups

| Group | Service | Example |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `compute` | Compute Engine | `gcloud compute instances list` |
| `storage` | Cloud Storage | `gcloud storage cp file gs://bucket/` |
| `functions` | Cloud Functions | `gcloud functions deploy NAME` |
| `iam` | IAM | `gcloud iam service-accounts list` |
| `projects` | Project management | `gcloud projects list` |
| `services` | API management | `gcloud services enable compute.googleapis.com` |
| `logging` | Cloud Logging | `gcloud logging read "severity>=ERROR"` |
| `run` | Cloud Run | `gcloud run deploy NAME --image=IMAGE` |
| `container` | GKE | `gcloud container clusters list` |
| `sql` | Cloud SQL | `gcloud sql instances list` |
| `secrets` | Secret Manager | `gcloud secrets create NAME --data-file=FILE` |

## Project Management

```bash
# List projects
gcloud projects list

# Create a new project
gcloud projects create my-new-project --name="My New Project"

# Set default project
gcloud config set project my-new-project

# Enable an API
gcloud services enable compute.googleapis.com
gcloud services enable cloudfunctions.googleapis.com
gcloud services enable storage.googleapis.com

# List enabled APIs
gcloud services list --enabled

# Link a billing account
gcloud billing projects link my-new-project \
  --billing-account=BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID
```

## Output Formatting

```bash
# JSON output
gcloud compute instances list --format=json

# YAML output
gcloud compute instances describe my-vm --format=yaml

# Table with specific columns
gcloud compute instances list --format="table(name, zone, status, networkInterfaces[0].accessConfigs[0].natIP)"

# Value only (for scripting)
gcloud compute instances describe my-vm --format="value(networkInterfaces[0].accessConfigs[0].natIP)"

# CSV output
gcloud compute instances list --format=csv

# Filter results
gcloud compute instances list --filter="status=RUNNING AND zone:us-central1-*"

# Limit results
gcloud compute instances list --limit=10
```

## Useful Flags

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| `--project=ID` | Override default project |
| `--format=json\|yaml\|table\|csv\|value` | Output format |
| `--filter=EXPR` | Server-side filtering |
| `--limit=N` | Limit number of results |
| `--quiet` | Suppress prompts (for scripting) |
| `--verbosity=debug` | Debug output |
| `--impersonate-service-account=SA` | Act as a service account |
| `--flatten=KEY` | Flatten nested arrays |
| `--sort-by=FIELD` | Sort output |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `CLOUDSDK_CORE_PROJECT` | Default project |
| `CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_REGION` | Default compute region |
| `CLOUDSDK_COMPUTE_ZONE` | Default compute zone |
| `CLOUDSDK_CORE_ACCOUNT` | Default account |
| `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` | Service account key path |
| `CLOUDSDK_AUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN` | Override auth with a token |
| `CLOUDSDK_CORE_DISABLE_PROMPTS` | Set to 1 for non-interactive mode |

sections.title: Installation
sections.body: Install via Homebrew (`brew install --cask google-cloud-sdk`), the official installer (`curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash`), or system package manager. After installation, run `gcloud init` for guided setup. Verify with `gcloud version`. The SDK includes gcloud, gsutil (Cloud Storage), and bq (BigQuery). Update with `gcloud components update`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Use `gcloud auth login` for interactive browser-based auth. Use `gcloud auth application-default login` to set ADC that client libraries auto-discover. For CI/CD, use `gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file=KEY`. Use `gcloud auth print-access-token` to get a Bearer token for REST API calls. List accounts with `gcloud auth list`. See [[gcp/iam]] for service account creation and role assignment.

sections.title: Configuration management
sections.body: Set defaults with `gcloud config set PROPERTY VALUE`. Key properties: `project` (project ID), `compute/region`, `compute/zone`, `account`. Named configurations let you switch between environments: `gcloud config configurations create NAME`, then activate with `gcloud config configurations activate NAME`. Override any default per-command with `--project`, `--region`, etc. View current config with `gcloud config list`.

sections.title: Output formatting and filtering
sections.body: Control output with `--format` (json, yaml, table, csv, value). Filter server-side with `--filter` using expressions like `status=RUNNING AND zone:us-central1-*`. For scripting, use `--format=value(FIELD)` to extract a single value. Flatten nested structures with `--flatten=KEY`. Sort with `--sort-by=FIELD`. Limit results with `--limit=N`. JSON output is best for programmatic parsing; table for human reading.

sections.title: Scripting and automation
sections.body: For non-interactive scripts: set `--quiet` to suppress prompts, use `--format=json` or `--format=value(FIELD)` for parseable output, set `CLOUDSDK_CORE_DISABLE_PROMPTS=1` environment variable. Use `--impersonate-service-account=SA_EMAIL` to act as a service account without downloading keys. Chain commands with `$(gcloud ...)` for dynamic values: `gcloud compute ssh $(gcloud compute instances list --limit=1 --format='value(name)') --zone=$(gcloud compute instances list --limit=1 --format='value(zone)')`.

links.context: IAM commands — roles, service accounts, policies
gcloud compute commands for VM management

related.note: IAM operations are performed through gcloud iam commands
gcloud compute is the primary interface for Compute Engine
gcloud storage replaces gsutil for Cloud Storage operations
gcloud functions deploy is how you deploy Cloud Functions
gcloud commands are run in bash/shell environments

scripts.name: gcloud CLI Setup
scripts.description: Install and configure the gcloud CLI with project, region, zone, and authentication

GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT.description: GCP project ID to configure as default

GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS.description: Path to service account key for non-interactive auth

key_file.description: Path to service account key file
